package handler

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

// Positive scenario for validation_logic_duplicated_across_handlers.
// Rule intent: Repeated required-field or enum-validation blocks across multiple handler files.

type CreateUserRequest struct {
	Name  string `json:"name" binding:"required"`
	Email string `json:"email" binding:"required"`
	Role  string `json:"role"`
}

type UpdateUserRequest struct {
	Name  string `json:"name" binding:"required"`
	Email string `json:"email" binding:"required"`
	Role  string `json:"role"`
}

type CreateOrderRequest struct {
	Name  string `json:"name" binding:"required"`
	Email string `json:"email" binding:"required"`
	Role  string `json:"role"`
}

// CreateUserHandler validates input with duplicated logic.
func CreateUserHandler(c *gin.Context) {
	var req CreateUserRequest
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&req); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	// Duplicated validation block #1
	if req.Name == "" {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "name is required"})
		return
	}
	if len(req.Name) > 100 {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "name must be less than 100 characters"})
		return
	}
	if req.Email == "" {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "email is required"})
		return
	}
	validRoles := map[string]bool{"admin": true, "user": true, "viewer": true}
	if !validRoles[req.Role] {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "invalid role"})
		return
	}

	c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"message": "user created"})
}

// UpdateUserHandler validates input with the SAME duplicated logic.
func UpdateUserHandler(c *gin.Context) {
	var req UpdateUserRequest
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&req); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	// Duplicated validation block #2 — identical to CreateUserHandler
	if req.Name == "" {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "name is required"})
		return
	}
	if len(req.Name) > 100 {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "name must be less than 100 characters"})
		return
	}
	if req.Email == "" {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "email is required"})
		return
	}
	validRoles := map[string]bool{"admin": true, "user": true, "viewer": true}
	if !validRoles[req.Role] {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "invalid role"})
		return
	}

	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"message": "user updated"})
}

// CreateOrderHandler also has the SAME duplicated validation logic.
func CreateOrderHandler(c *gin.Context) {
	var req CreateOrderRequest
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&req); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	// Duplicated validation block #3 — identical to the other two handlers
	if req.Name == "" {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "name is required"})
		return
	}
	if len(req.Name) > 100 {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "name must be less than 100 characters"})
		return
	}
	if req.Email == "" {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "email is required"})
		return
	}
	validRoles := map[string]bool{"admin": true, "user": true, "viewer": true}
	if !validRoles[req.Role] {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "invalid role"})
		return
	}

	c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"message": "order created"})
}
